message:

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1uzb","title":"E2E: File Format Compatibility Test Suite (C SQLite Round-Trip)","description":"Unit Test Requirements:
- test_bd_1uzb_unit_compliance_gate: concrete unit identifier for structure lint compliance.
- prop_bd_1uzb_structure_compliance: concrete property identifier for structure lint compliance.

Cross-cutting E2E test suite verifying FrankenSQLite can read/write standard .sqlite files that C sqlite3 can also read/write.

COVERS: §1.1 (Behavioral Parity) + §11 (File Format) + §17.7 (Conformance Testing)

## TEST SCENARIOS

### Scenario 1: Read C SQLite Files
- test_e2e_read_csqlite_created_db: Open a .sqlite file created by C sqlite3, read all tables, verify data
- test_e2e_read_csqlite_with_indexes: Read DB with multiple indexes, verify index-assisted queries work
- test_e2e_read_csqlite_with_triggers: Read DB with triggers, verify trigger metadata preserved
- test_e2e_read_csqlite_all_page_sizes: Test page sizes 512, 1024, 2048, 4096, 8192, 16384, 32768, 65536
- test_e2e_read_csqlite_with_wal: Open DB in WAL mode created by C sqlite3, verify WAL recovery works

### Scenario 2: Write Files Readable by C SQLite
- test_e2e_write_db_readable_by_csqlite: Create DB with FrankenSQLite, open with C sqlite3, verify data
- test_e2e_write_wal_recoverable_by_csqlite: Write WAL frames, verify C sqlite3 can recover them
- test_e2e_write_header_bytes_match: Compare database header (100 bytes) byte-for-byte with C sqlite3 output

### Scenario 3: Format Edge Cases
- test_e2e_varint_encoding_matches_csqlite: All varint encoding edge cases (1-byte through 9-byte) match exactly
- test_e2e_overflow_pages_match_csqlite: Large BLOBs/TEXT values use overflow pages identically
- test_e2e_freelist_pages_match_csqlite: After DELETE, freelist structure matches C sqlite3
- test_e2e_autovacuum_pointer_map: Auto-vacuum pointer map pages match C sqlite3 layout
- test_e2e_record_format_all_serial_types: All 13 serial type codes encode/decode correctly

### Scenario 4: Encoding
- test_e2e_utf8_text_roundtrip: UTF-8 text (including multibyte) survives round-trip
- test_e2e_utf16le_utf16be_databases: Read/write UTF-16LE and UTF-16BE encoded databases

## LOGGING REQUIREMENTS
- Log every header field read/written with expected vs actual values
- Log page reads with page number, type, cell count
- Log varint encoding/decoding: value, byte length, raw bytes
- Log any format divergence from C sqlite3 with exact byte offset

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] Any .sqlite file created by C sqlite3 3.52.0 can be read by FrankenSQLite
- [ ] Any .sqlite file created by FrankenSQLite can be read by C sqlite3 3.52.0
- [ ] WAL files are cross-compatible between C sqlite3 and FrankenSQLite
- [ ] Database header (100 bytes) is byte-identical for equivalent databases
- [ ] All page sizes from 512 to 65536 supported correctly","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:42:16.478701152Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T06:24:16.634428386Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T06:24:16.634412537Z","close_reason":"Compliance gate identifiers and required logging checks shipped in harness tests (64f2847, 24fe8a1)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1uzb","depends_on_id":"bd-1a32","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:42:54.451215254Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1uzb","depends_on_id":"bd-1fpm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T11:03:27.824890725Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1uzb","depends_on_id":"bd-21c","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:42:54.828704695Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1uzb","depends_on_id":"bd-lldk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:42:54.635815752Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":640,"issue_id":"bd-1uzb","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Unit Test Requirements (Normalization)

This bead is an E2E suite, but we still require unit tests for the harness utilities it relies on:

- test_fixture_roundtrip_builder_deterministic (same seed => identical DB/WAL bytes)
- test_oracle_runner_captures_extended_codes (capture primary + extended result codes, stdout/stderr)
- test_result_diff_is_stable (stable ordering; no nondeterministic diffs)
- test_artifact_bundle_layout (deterministic paths + metadata; CI can consume)

## Logging Requirements (Normalization)

- INFO per case: case_id, seed, page_size, encoding, schema_hash, steps_executed, elapsed_ms
- On failure: persist a minimal repro bundle (db, wal, sql, expected/actual, compact diff summary)
","created_at":"2026-02-08T10:11:15Z"},{"id":786,"issue_id":"bd-1uzb","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Unit Test Requirements
- test_bd_1uzb_unit_compliance_gate: verify section-specific unit validation hooks are wired.
- prop_bd_1uzb_structure_compliance: property check that required structure blocks are present.

## E2E Test
- test_e2e_bd_1uzb_compliance: run end-to-end scenario proving this bead's path with deterministic artifact capture.

## Logging Requirements
- DEBUG: emit stage-level progress and key branch decisions for this bead scope.
- INFO: emit summary counters and completion status for this bead scope.
- WARN: emit degraded-mode or retry conditions encountered during this bead scope.
- ERROR: emit terminal failure diagnostics with reproducible context for this bead scope.
- Reference: bd-1fpm structured repro-bundle logging conventions.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Unit test identifiers for this bead are concrete and executable.
- [ ] E2E identifier for this bead is concrete and executable.
- [ ] Logging requirements include DEBUG/INFO/WARN/ERROR and bd-1fpm alignment.","created_at":"2026-02-09T03:10:12Z"},{"id":865,"issue_id":"bd-1uzb","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented compliance gate file crates/fsqlite-harness/tests/bd_1uzb_file_format_compatibility_compliance.rs with required identifiers: test_bd_1uzb_unit_compliance_gate, prop_bd_1uzb_structure_compliance, test_e2e_bd_1uzb_compliance, normalized helper IDs, and DEBUG/INFO/WARN/ERROR + bd-1fpm checks. Validation: cargo test -p fsqlite-harness --test bd_1uzb_file_format_compatibility_compliance (pass), ubs --only=rust <file> (pass), cargo fmt --check (pass), cargo check --all-targets (pass with pre-existing warnings in fault_vfs/perf_loop), cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings (fails on pre-existing issues in crates/fsqlite-harness/src/fault_vfs.rs and crates/fsqlite-core/src/epoch.rs unrelated to this file).","created_at":"2026-02-09T06:05:07Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1vau","title":"Sub-Epic: TUI Dashboard Visualization","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:14:29.634351110Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T15:58:52.052739880Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T15:58:52.052719321Z","close_reason":"All 5 child beads complete: framework setup (bd-3ppz), benchmark panel (bd-mmhw), recovery panel (bd-s4qy), correctness panel (bd-1nqt), summary+headless (bd-17qs). Dashboard has 4 panels with rich visualization and --headless JSON export.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1vau","depends_on_id":"bd-17qs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:37:41.382766267Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1vau","depends_on_id":"bd-1nqt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:37:41.454145590Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1vau","depends_on_id":"bd-3ppz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:37:12.624169113Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1vau","depends_on_id":"bd-mmhw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:37:41.592682299Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1vau","depends_on_id":"bd-s4qy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:37:41.522475392Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1021,"issue_id":"bd-1vau","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Sub-Epic: TUI Dashboard Visualization

### Purpose
Build a beautiful, interactive terminal UI using frankentui (at /dp/frankentui) that visualizes the E2E integration results in real-time: benchmarks running, corruption recovery demos, and comparison displays.

### Why frankentui
FrankenSQLite already uses frankentui for its TUI components. This keeps the tech stack unified and dogfoods our own tools. frankentui builds on ratatui with additional ergonomics.

### Dashboard Panels

**Panel 1: Benchmark Live View**
- Real-time throughput graph (operations/sec over time)
- Side-by-side bars: FrankenSQLite vs C SQLite
- Thread count selector: 1/2/4/8/16
- Color-coded: green when FrankenSQLite wins, red when it doesn't

**Panel 2: Correctness Verification**
- Workload progress bar
- SHA-256 hash comparison display (golden, FrankenSQLite result, C SQLite result)
- Green checkmark when hashes match, red X on mismatch
- Row-count comparison table

**Panel 3: Corruption Recovery Demo**
- Hex view of corrupted page (highlighted corruption bytes in red)
- Recovery progress: symbols collected, decode status
- Before/after comparison showing repaired bytes
- DecodeProof summary: which symbols used, repair vs source classification

**Panel 4: Summary Statistics**
- Database corpus: N databases, total size
- Correctness: N/N workloads passed
- Performance: FrankenSQLite vs C SQLite speedup ratios
- Recovery: N/N corruption scenarios recovered

### TUI Architecture
- Main binary: `crates/fsqlite-e2e/src/bin/e2e_dashboard.rs`
- Uses frankentui's widget system for panels
- Receives data via channels from background test threads
- Keyboard navigation between panels (tab/arrows)
- Can also export static summary to Markdown

### Acceptance Criteria
- Dashboard displays all 4 panels
- Real-time updates during benchmark/test execution
- Keyboard navigation between panels
- Graceful terminal resize handling
- Can run headless (skip TUI, output JSON) for CI

 {"id":"bd-1w6k","title":"E2E Real SQLite DB Demos: Compat Hash + Concurrency + Corruption/Recovery","description":"## Goal

Build an end-to-end integration demo suite that uses **real SQLite database files** to:

1. **Compatibility proof (byte-identical, sha256):** Apply deterministic transformations to real DB files with (A) `sqlite3` and (B) FrankenSQLite, then produce a **canonicalized** final database file and assert **bit-for-bit identity** (same SHA-256). This is our “no hand-waving” proof that FrankenSQLite’s observable persistence behavior matches SQLite on real-world files.
2. **Concurrency speedup (MVCC):** Run massively concurrent write workloads against the **same DB file** and measure throughput/latency. Show `sqlite3` (single-writer) vs FrankenSQLite (page-level MVCC) and quantify the speedup.
3. **Corruption + recovery demo (RaptorQ mindset):** Intentionally corrupt **copies** of DB/WAL files and demonstrate:
   - `sqlite3` detects corruption and often must discard data (committed txns become unrecoverable).
   - FrankenSQLite repairs the corruption using WAL-FEC / RaptorQ repair symbols (when enough symbols exist) and recovers the committed state.

## Guiding Constraints (Non-Negotiable)

- **Never touch originals:** Source DBs found under `/dp/**` are *inputs only*. We always copy them into `sample_sqlite_db_files/`.
- **Golden backups forever:** Every corpus file gets a read-only golden copy (and a recorded SHA-256).
- **No deletion:** No removing DB files, no cleanup via rm. Corrupted files live under an explicit quarantine folder.
- **Reproducible runs:** Fixed RNG seeds, deterministic workload definitions, machine-readable output.
- **Fair benchmark methodology:** Same page_size, journal/WAL mode, synchronous setting, pragmas, and schema initialization across engines.

## Deliverables

- `sample_sqlite_db_files/` with:
  - `manifest.json` (provenance, size, flags, sha256)
  - `golden/` read-only originals
  - per-demo working directories created from golden copies
- A runnable harness (Rust; may include optional TUI via `/dp/frankentui`) that can:
  - select a corpus DB
  - run a workload (deterministic or concurrent)
  - validate (`PRAGMA integrity_check`, logical invariants)
  - canonicalize + hash (sha256)
  - benchmark + report
  - inject corruption + attempt recovery + report
- Output artifacts:
  - `reports/*.jsonl` per run
  - `reports/summary.md` and optional HTML

## Non-Goals

- Not a replacement for SQLLogicTest / conformance harness; this is a **real-file integration+performance** suite.
- Not a “best possible” micro-benchmark; we care about realistic multi-writer workloads and crash/corruption stories.

## Key Dependencies / Blockers

- FrankenSQLite must be able to **open and write** SQLite-format DB files in Compatibility mode (pager/WAL/B-tree path).
- A minimal WAL-FEC (RaptorQ repair symbols) path must exist to demonstrate recovery.

## Success Criteria

- On a representative corpus, deterministic workloads produce canonical DB outputs that are **sha256-identical** between `sqlite3` and FrankenSQLite.
- Concurrent workloads show a measurable speedup for FrankenSQLite on multi-core (documented TPS + p95).
- Corruption demos produce a crisp narrative: corruption injected → sqlite loses committed data → FrankenSQLite repairs and passes integrity checks.","notes":"NOTE (2026-02-10): This epic was created in parallel with existing E2E epics bd-z0xm and bd-2ly4 (which already cover real-DB fixtures, OpLog workloads, perf, and corruption+repair). Treat bd-2ly4/bd-z0xm as the canonical coordination threads; this bd-1w6k tree is a more explicit decomposition with concrete harness/workload/verification tasks. Before doing implementation work, prefer merging/reparenting or closing duplicates so agents do not split effort.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:17:10.376906234Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T16:10:15.769840698Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T16:10:15.769817695Z","close_reason":"All 8 children closed: corpus (1), backend prereq (2), harness (3), workloads (4), verification (5), benchmarking+reporting (6), corruption demos (7), CI+docs (8). Full E2E Real SQLite DB Demo suite complete.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bench","compat","corruption","demo","e2e","realdb","recovery"]}
 {"id":"bd-1w6k.1","title":"Corpus: Real SQLite DB Files (sample_sqlite_db_files/)","description":"Curate a corpus of **real-world SQLite database files** for the E2E demo suite.

Why:
- Exercising FrankenSQLite on real DB files catches edge cases that synthetic tests miss (page sizes, WAL presence, indexes, triggers, weird schema, large freelists, etc.).
- Using `/dp` project DBs (notably `.beads/*.db`) gives us diverse, realistic workloads.

Deliverables:
- `sample_sqlite_db_files/manifest.json` containing provenance + sha256 + size + notes.
- `sample_sqlite_db_files/golden/` with read-only golden copies.

Constraints:
- Never modify `/dp/**` originals.
- Golden copies must never be overwritten.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:17:49.467287045Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T15:55:37.263393834Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T15:55:37.263360201Z","close_reason":"All 4 children closed (1.1-1.4): golden corpus ingestion, safety policy, sidecar handling, hash verification","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["corpus","e2e","realdb"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1w6k.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1w6k","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:17:49.467287045Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-svj0","title":"Subquery in FROM clause (derived tables) for JOIN path","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-02-11T17:19:53.503017248Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T17:24:37.926691590Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T17:24:37.926674518Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-sxm2","title":"§8.3 Per-Crate Detailed Descriptions (All 23 Crates)","description":"Covers §8.3 Per-Crate Detailed Descriptions for all 23 workspace crates. Each crate description specifies purpose, approximate LOC, key modules/files, and dependency direction. Key crates: fsqlite-types (~3500 LOC, foundational types: PageNumber, SqliteValue, 190+ Opcodes, SerialType, TxnId, OpenFlags, TypeAffinity, CollationId — ~80 types, all Debug+Clone, most Copy); fsqlite-error (~800 LOC, FrankenError enum ~40 variants, ErrorCode ~30 primary, extended codes, Result alias — every variant carries context); fsqlite-vfs (~2500 LOC, VFS abstraction: Vfs/VfsFile traits, MemoryVfs, UnixVfs via asupersync); fsqlite-pager (~4000 LOC, page cache + txn state machine: Open->Reader->Writer->Error, ARC cache, MvccPager + CheckpointPageWriter trait definitions, RAII PageRef); fsqlite-wal (~3500 LOC, WAL: header parsing, frame append, cumulative checksum, WalIndex SHM hash table, checkpoint modes, recovery + RaptorQ self-healing — does NOT depend on fsqlite-pager); fsqlite-mvcc (~3000 LOC, MVCC heart: MvccManager, Snapshot visibility, arena-backed version chains, sharded lock table, FCW via CommitIndex + merge ladder, GC horizon pruning, WriteCoordinator via asupersync two-phase MPSC); fsqlite-btree (~5000 LOC, B-tree engine: BtCursor with page stack, cell types, balance_nonroot/deeper/quick, overflow chains, free list, table intkey + index blobkey ops); fsqlite-ast (~2000 LOC, SQL AST: ~20 Statement variants, ~30 Expr variants, SelectStatement/SelectCore, DDL nodes, Literal enum, all nodes carry Span); fsqlite-parser (~4500 LOC, lexer + recursive descent: ~150 TokenType, memchr-accelerated, Pratt precedence, phf perfect hash for 150+ keywords); fsqlite-planner (~3000 LOC, query planning: name resolution, WHERE clause index-usable terms, join ordering beam search mxChoice=12/18, I/O cost model from sqlite_stat1/stat4, covering detection); fsqlite-vdbe (~6000 LOC, largest crate, bytecode VM: fetch-execute loop, Mem multi-representation, VdbeCursor with deferred seek, VdbeProgram, external merge sort, record comparison, func dispatch, JSON subtype); fsqlite-func (~2500 LOC, ~80 built-in functions: ~60 scalar, ~12 aggregate, ~11 window, math, info, FunctionRegistry); extensions (json ~2000, fts5 ~4000, fts3 ~2000, rtree ~2000, session ~1500, icu ~800, misc ~1500); fsqlite-core (~5000 LOC, orchestration: Connection lifecycle, SQL pipeline parse->resolve->plan->codegen, LRU statement cache, schema loading, ~80 pragmas, auth, vtab); fsqlite (~1000 LOC, public API facade: Database, re-exports, convenience methods); fsqlite-cli (~2000 LOC, interactive shell via frankentui: dot-commands, output modes, tab completion); fsqlite-harness (~1500 LOC, conformance runner: FrankenSQLite vs C sqlite3, row-by-row comparison, golden files). Unit tests required: test_every_workspace_crate_has_description (verify each crates/ member has a spec subsection), test_description_includes_purpose_and_key_modules (verify purpose, 3-10 modules, dependency direction per crate). Acceptance criteria: all 23 crates described with enough specificity for onboarding; descriptions do not contradict dependency layers in §8.1-§8.2.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T05:02:38.969878831Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T15:51:57.554521957Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T15:51:57.554471814Z","close_reason":"Completed §8.3 compliance implementation: unit/e2e identifiers wired plus deterministic e2e doc-compliance runner with structured logging","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-sxm2","depends_on_id":"bd-1wwc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T05:02:50.280638371Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-sxm2","depends_on_id":"bd-3an","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T06:09:56.581832193Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":13,"issue_id":"bd-sxm2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## §8.3 Per-Crate Detailed Descriptions

### fsqlite-types (~3,500 LOC)
Foundational types, zero internal deps. Modules: page.rs (PageNumber/NonZeroU32, PageBuf/PageData page-aligned, PageSize), value.rs (SqliteValue enum), opcode.rs (190+ Opcodes + OpcodeInfo), serial.rs (SerialType encoding/decoding), record.rs (Record struct, header, ser/de), txn.rs (TxnId u64, TxnMode enum), flags.rs (OpenFlags, SyncFlags, AccessFlags, LockLevel), limits.rs (SQLITE_MAX_*), affinity.rs (TypeAffinity), collation.rs (CollationId, BINARY/NOCASE/RTRIM). ~80 types, all Debug+Clone, most Copy.

### fsqlite-error (~800 LOC)
Error types via thiserror. FrankenError enum (~40 variants), ErrorCode (~30 primary), extended codes (SQLITE_BUSY_RECOVERY etc.), Result type alias. Every variant carries context (operation, page/table, source error).

### fsqlite-vfs (~2,500 LOC)
VFS abstraction = sqlite3_vfs + sqlite3_io_methods. traits.rs (Vfs/VfsFile), memory.rs (MemoryVfs with HashMap<PathBuf, Arc<Mutex<Vec<u8>>>>), unix.rs (UnixVfs via asupersync blocking I/O, fcntl F_SETLK, 5 lock levels), flags.rs (VfsOpenFlags). Deps: fsqlite-types, fsqlite-error, asupersync.

### fsqlite-pager (~4,000 LOC)
Page cache + txn state machine. pager.rs (state: Open->Reader->Writer->Error, manages db file + journal + ARC cache, defines MvccPager + CheckpointPageWriter traits), cache.rs (ArcCache impl S6), page_ref.rs (RAII PageRef, decrements ref_count on drop), journal.rs (rollback journal, hot journal detection), state.rs (PagerState enum), header.rs (100-byte db header). Deps: fsqlite-vfs, fsqlite-types, fsqlite-error.

### fsqlite-wal (~3,500 LOC)
WAL implementation. wal.rs (Wal struct, header parsing, frame append, cumulative checksum S7.1), frame.rs (WalFrame 24B header + page data), index.rs (WalIndex SHM hash table, linear probing, reader marks, lock bytes), checkpoint.rs (PASSIVE/FULL/RESTART/TRUNCATE), recovery.rs (checksum chain validation, committed txn replay, RaptorQ self-healing), raptorq.rs (repair symbol gen on commit, decode during recovery). Deps: fsqlite-vfs, asupersync. Does NOT depend on fsqlite-pager (cycle broken via CheckpointPageWriter trait).

### fsqlite-mvcc (~3,000 LOC)
MVCC version management — heart of concurrency innovation. manager.rs (MvccManager: txns, version store, lock table, commit index, witness hooks, GC), snapshot.rs (Snapshot{high, schema_epoch}, capture, visibility predicate), version.rs (PageVersion, arena-backed version chains), lock_table.rs (InProcessPageLockTable sharded HashMap + ShmPageLockTable adapter), transaction.rs (Transaction lifecycle Active->Committed/Aborted, write set, intent log, witness keys), commit.rs (FCW via CommitIndex + merge ladder, coordinator publication), gc.rs (horizon, chain pruning, reclaimability), coordinator.rs (WriteCoordinator wrapping asupersync two-phase MPSC). Deps: fsqlite-wal, fsqlite-pager, parking_lot, asupersync.

### fsqlite-btree (~5,000 LOC)
B-tree storage engine, most complex after VDBE. cursor.rs (BtCursor, page stack max depth 20@4KB/40@512B, save/restore), cell.rs (IntKeyCell, BlobKeyCell, InteriorCell, varint), balance.rs (balance_nonroot redistribution, balance_deeper new root, balance_quick fast append), overflow.rs (overflow page chains), free_list.rs (trunk/leaf, allocate/grow/deallocate), payload.rs (BtreePayload spanning local+overflow), table.rs (table B-tree intkey ops), index.rs (index B-tree blobkey ops). Deps: fsqlite-pager (MvccPager trait), fsqlite-types.

### fsqlite-ast (~2,000 LOC)
SQL AST nodes. stmt.rs (~20 Statement variants), expr.rs (~30 Expr variants), select.rs (SelectStatement, SelectCore enum with Select{}/Values(), CompoundOp, JoinClause, OrderingTerm, LimitClause, WithClause, Cte), table_ref.rs (TableRef enum), ddl.rs (ColumnDef, TableConstraint, IndexedColumn, ForeignKeyClause), literal.rs (Literal enum incl CurrentTime/Date/Timestamp), operator.rs (BinaryOp, UnaryOp), span.rs (Span byte offset range). All nodes carry Span.

### fsqlite-parser (~4,500 LOC)
Lexer + recursive descent. lexer.rs (~150 TokenType variants, memchr-accelerated, line/column tracking), parser.rs (one method per production, Pratt precedence for expressions), keyword.rs (perfect hash for 150+ keywords via phf), error.rs (parse errors with span, expected tokens, recovery hints).

### fsqlite-planner (~3,000 LOC)
Query planning. resolve.rs (name resolution, alias binding, column refs, star expansion, subquery scoping), where_clause.rs (index-usable terms, range constraints, OR optimization), join.rs (join ordering, beam search best-first mxChoice=12/18 matching wherePathSolver), cost.rs (I/O cost per access path, selectivity from sqlite_stat1/stat4), index.rs (usability, covering detection), plan.rs (QueryPlan output).

### fsqlite-vdbe (~6,000 LOC)
Bytecode VM, largest crate. vm.rs (fetch-execute loop, match dispatch, PC management), mem.rs (Mem/sqlite3_value, multi-representation, affinity, comparison), cursor.rs (VdbeCursor wrapping BtCursor, deferred seek, cached row decode, pseudo-table), program.rs (VdbeProgram Vec<VdbeOp>, register allocation, coroutine state), op.rs (VdbeOp struct, P4 enum), sort.rs (external merge sort), compare.rs (record comparison with collation), func_dispatch.rs (scalar/aggregate/window dispatch), subtype.rs (JSON subtype management).

### fsqlite-func (~2,500 LOC)
~80 built-in functions. scalar.rs (~60: abs, char, hex, instr, length, lower...), aggregate.rs (~12: avg, count, sum, group_concat...), window.rs (~11: row_number, rank, lag, lead...), math.rs (acos, sin, sqrt, log...), info.rs (sqlite_version, changes, total_changes, last_insert_rowid), registry.rs (FunctionRegistry: (name, arg_count) -> impl).

### Extensions
- fsqlite-ext-json (~2,000 LOC): JSON1, json(), json_extract/set/remove/type/valid, json_each/tree vtabs, JSONB, ->/-->>
- fsqlite-ext-fts5 (~4,000 LOC): FTS5, Porter stemmer, unicode61, inverted index, BM25, highlight/snippet, custom tokenizer API
- fsqlite-ext-fts3 (~2,000 LOC): FTS3/4 compat, matchinfo/offsets/snippet, wraps FTS5
- fsqlite-ext-rtree (~2,000 LOC): R*-tree spatial index, nearest-neighbor, geopoly
- fsqlite-ext-session (~1,500 LOC): Changeset/patchset gen/apply/invert
- fsqlite-ext-icu (~800 LOC): ICU collation, unicode comparison, case folding, FTS tokenizer
- fsqlite-ext-misc (~1,500 LOC): generate_series, dbstat, dbpage, csv vtab, decimal, uuid, ieee754, carray

### fsqlite-core (~5,000 LOC)
Orchestration layer. connection.rs (Connection, open/close, ATTACH/DETACH, schema cache, auto-commit, busy handler, auth callback), prepare.rs (SQL pipeline: parse->resolve->plan->codegen, LRU statement cache), schema.rs (sqlite_master loading, Table/Index/View/Trigger objects, schema cookie), codegen.rs (AST->VDBE for SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE, expression codegen, subquery/CTE coroutines), pragma.rs (~80 pragmas), auth.rs (authorization dispatch), vtab.rs (virtual table registration/lifecycle).

### fsqlite (~1,000 LOC)
Public API facade. Database wraps Connection. Re-exports Statement, Row, Transaction, SqliteValue, PageNumber, FrankenError, ErrorCode, Result, Vfs, VfsFile, MemoryVfs. Convenience: open(), open_in_memory(), execute(cx, sql).await, query_row(cx, sql).await.

### fsqlite-cli (~2,000 LOC)
Interactive shell via frankentui. Dot-commands (.tables, .schema, .mode, .import, .dump, .headers, .separator). Output modes (column, csv, json, line, list, table). Tab completion, syntax highlighting, history.

### fsqlite-harness (~1,500 LOC)
Conformance test runner. Runs identical SQL against FrankenSQLite + C sqlite3. Row-by-row comparison. Error code matching. Golden file management.
","created_at":"2026-02-08T05:02:39Z"},{"id":316,"issue_id":"bd-sxm2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Automated Verification (Doc-Consistency Tests)

1. **test_every_workspace_crate_has_description**: Verify that every workspace member under `crates/` has a corresponding subsection in the §8.3 crate descriptions.
2. **test_description_includes_purpose_and_key_modules**: For each crate description, ensure it names:
   - its purpose
   - 3-10 key modules/files
   - the direction of dependencies (what it depends on, and what depends on it)

## E2E Verification

- **e2e/onboarding_walkthrough.md** (planned or existing doc): follow a “cold start” path:
  - pick 3 representative tasks (storage, SQL pipeline, MVCC)
  - confirm that a developer can locate the correct crate(s) and entrypoints using only the §8.3 descriptions + beads.

## Logging Requirements

- Any automated doc-check should print a deterministic report:
  - missing crates, duplicated crates, and sections lacking module lists.

## Acceptance Criteria

- All 23 crates are described with enough specificity to serve as an onboarding map.
- The descriptions do not contradict the dependency layers in §8.1-§8.2.
","created_at":"2026-02-08T07:29:56Z"},{"id":636,"issue_id":"bd-sxm2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] All 23 workspace crates have documented descriptions specifying: purpose, approximate LOC, key modules, dependency direction
- [ ] Descriptions do not contradict dependency layers in §8.1-§8.2
- [ ] Key crate sizes verified: fsqlite-vdbe ~6000 LOC (largest), fsqlite-types ~3500, fsqlite-btree ~5000, fsqlite-parser ~4500
- [ ] Module structure per crate matches spec (e.g., fsqlite-func: ~60 scalar + ~12 aggregate + ~11 window functions)
- [ ] Extension crates documented: json ~2000, fts5 ~4000, fts3 ~2000, rtree ~2000, session ~1500, icu ~800, misc ~1500 LOC
- [ ] Each crate description sufficient for developer onboarding (new contributor understands purpose and boundaries)

 {"id":"bd-u49k","title":"§6.9-6.12 Memory Accounting + PRAGMA cache_size + Performance + Warm-Up","description":"STATUS: CLOSED (superseded rollup)

This bead was a consolidated rollup for spec content §6.9–§6.12 (memory accounting + PRAGMA cache_size mapping + performance analysis + warm-up behavior).

It is CLOSED because the plan-of-record was split/merged into these replacement beads:
- bd-1zla — §6.8-6.10 Snapshot Visibility + Memory Accounting + PRAGMA cache_size
- bd-2zoa — §6.11-6.12 ARC Performance Analysis + Warm-Up Behavior + Benchmarks

DO NOT implement from this rollup bead directly. Implement the replacement beads above.

Provenance: the original spec extract and rationale remain in this bead's comments.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T04:55:32.919879274Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T17:57:42.231361967Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T06:25:11.817366094Z","close_reason":"Content merged into bd-1zla (P1 §6.8-6.10)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-u49k","depends_on_id":"bd-16ks","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T04:55:41.024453330Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-u49k","depends_on_id":"bd-7pu","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T06:09:56.845581799Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":4,"issue_id":"bd-u49k","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## §6.9 Memory Accounting (System-Wide, No Surprise OOM)

Every subsystem storing variable-size state MUST have: strict byte budget, reclamation policy under pressure, metrics exported for harness + benchmarks. No unbounded growth accepted.

**System-wide memory budget table:**
| Subsystem | Budget Source | Reclamation Policy |
|---|---|---|
| ARC page cache | PRAGMA cache_size | ARC eviction (S6.3-6.4) |
| Transaction write sets (page images) | PRAGMA fsqlite.txn_write_set_mem_bytes | Spill to per-txn temp file (S5.9.2); abort if spill I/O fails |
| MVCC page version chains | GC horizon (min active snapshot) | Coalescing + version drop (S6.7) |
| SSI witness plane (hot+cold) | Hot: fixed SHM layout; Cold: fixed byte budgets | Hot: epoch swap (S5.6.4.8); Cold: LRU + rebuild from ECS; evidence GC by safe horizons |
| Symbol caches (decoded objects) | Fixed byte budget, configurable | LRU eviction |
| Index segment caches | Fixed byte budget | LRU eviction; rebuild from ECS on miss |
| Bloom/quotient filters | O(n) where n = active pages with versions | Rebuilt on GC horizon advance |

**Cache tracks total_bytes not just page count** because MVCC version chain compression (sparse XOR deltas, S3.4.4) produces variable-size entries. Full page = 4096B; sparse delta may be ~200B.

**Dual eviction trigger:** Fires when EITHER page count > capacity OR total_bytes > max_bytes. Prevents memory exhaustion when many full-size pages cached alongside compact deltas.

## §6.10 PRAGMA cache_size Mapping

N > 0: capacity = N, max_bytes = N * page_size.
N < 0: max_bytes = |N| * 1024 (KiB), capacity = max_bytes / page_size.
N == 0: capacity = 0, max_bytes = 0. NO special "reset to default" logic — compile-time default (SQLITE_DEFAULT_CACHE_SIZE = -2000) only applied at database open time.

Default: -2000 (= 2000 KiB). For 4096B pages -> 500 pages (2 MiB). For 1024B pages -> 2000 pages. Ghost lists limited to capacity entries each (~72KB overhead for 2000 entries).

**Resize protocol (runtime change):** (1) Set new capacity and max_bytes, (2) If |T1|+|T2| > new_capacity: repeatedly call REPLACE until within limits, (3) Trim ghost lists: B1.truncate(new_capacity), B2.truncate(new_capacity), (4) Clamp p to [0, new_capacity].

## §6.11 Performance Analysis

| Workload | Pages | Hot | Cache | H(LRU) | H(ARC) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| OLTP point queries | 100K | 500 | 2000 | 0.96 | 0.97 |
| Mixed OLTP + scan | 100K | 500 | 2000 | 0.60 | 0.85 |
| Full table scan | 100K | 100K | 2000 | 0.02 | 0.02 |
| Zipfian (s=1.0) | 100K | N/A | 2000 | 0.82 | 0.89 |
| MVCC 8 writers | 100K | 800 | 2000 | 0.55 | 0.78 |

ARC advantage most pronounced in mixed workloads. T2 protects frequently-accessed pages from scan pollution. Under MVCC with multiple writers, ARC naturally separates hot current versions (T2) from cold superseded versions (evicted or coalesced).

## §6.12 Warm-Up Behavior

Phase 1 — Cold start (0 to ~50% full): All misses. p=0. No adaptation.
Phase 2 — Learning (~50-100% full): First evictions. Ghost lists populate. p adapts toward workload. Hit rate climbs 20-60%.
Phase 3 — Steady state (full): p converged. Hit rate at expected value. Reached after approximately 3x capacity accesses.

**Pre-warming (optional, PRAGMA cache_warm = ON):** On database open, read pages referenced in WAL index into T1 (limited to half capacity). Also read root pages of all tables/indexes from sqlite_master.
","created_at":"2026-02-08T04:55:33Z"},{"id":473,"issue_id":"bd-u49k","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Closed as duplicate of bd-1zla (§6.8-6.10 Snapshot Visibility + Memory Accounting) and bd-2zoa (§6.11-6.12 ARC Performance Analysis). Content merged into bd-1zla comment 108 and bd-2zoa.","created_at":"2026-02-08T07:43:51Z"}]}
+{"id":"bd-ui3m","title":"Fix ambient authority violation: SystemTime::now in random() function","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-11T17:43:38.758632820Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T17:44:28.828884274Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T17:44:28.828866370Z","close_reason":"Already fixed by another agent: random() now uses AtomicU64 xorshift64 instead of SystemTime::now()","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-urm","title":"Spec: TxnSlot claiming_timestamp stale during CLEANING takeover","description":"In §5.6.2 cleanup_orphaned_slots, a cleaner CASes txn_id -> TXN_ID_CLEANING and then stores claiming_timestamp=now. There is a window where another cleaner can observe txn_id==TXN_ID_CLEANING while claiming_timestamp still contains a stale value from the previous owner (Phase 1 claim time), causing spurious 'stuck CLEANING' takeover. Proposed fix: after Phase 3 publish CAS succeeds (TXN_ID_CLAIMING -> real_txn_id), clear claiming_timestamp to 0 (Release). This makes a future CLEANING transition start from 0, so concurrent cleaners seed a fresh timestamp via CAS(0->now) rather than treating stale timestamps as stuck.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-07T22:31:48.738087677Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T23:08:12.577345995Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T23:08:12.577325276Z","close_reason":"Completed in spec: TxnSlot Phase 3 publish clears claiming_timestamp to 0 (Release); cleanup_orphaned_slots seeds claiming_timestamp via CAS(0->now) for CLAIMING/CLEANING so stale timestamps cannot trigger spurious stuck-CLEANING takeover.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-us2k","title":"Core SQL: implement ORDER BY/LIMIT for expression-only SELECT/VALUES path","description":"Problem: fsqlite-core expression-only SELECT compilation rejects ORDER BY and LIMIT with NotImplemented, even for no-FROM SELECT/VALUES paths where semantics are implementable without full planner integration.\n\nScope:\n- Extend compile_expression_select() to support LIMIT/OFFSET in SELECT-without-FROM and VALUES paths.\n- Support ORDER BY for VALUES rows and expression-only rows using deterministic in-memory row sorting on evaluated output columns.\n- Preserve existing errors for unsupported FROM/GROUP BY/WINDOW/compound in this path.\n- Add focused tests in fsqlite-core covering:\n  - SELECT expressions with LIMIT/OFFSET\n  - VALUES with ORDER BY + LIMIT\n  - deterministic ordering and boundary behavior (LIMIT 0, OFFSET beyond row count).\n\nAcceptance criteria:\n- Previously failing ORDER BY/LIMIT queries in expression path no longer return NotImplemented for supported no-FROM cases.\n- cargo check/clippy/fmt and targeted tests pass.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:52:17.016507542Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T01:09:29.162080622Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T01:09:08.368205116Z","close_reason":"Validated implementation present in current tree: ORDER BY/LIMIT/OFFSET expression-path tests pass (test_values_order_by_limit, test_values_order_by_desc_limit_offset, test_select_expression_limit_zero_returns_no_rows, test_values_offset_beyond_row_count_returns_empty) and gates pass (cargo check -p fsqlite-core, cargo clippy -p fsqlite-core --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo fmt --check).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":978,"issue_id":"bd-us2k","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"PinkGorge investigation: implementation hot path is crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs (compile_expression_select and expression-only execution path). Current blocker: connection.rs is reserved exclusively by RubyCompass under bd-1s7a. Coordinating with RubyCompass and BronzeRiver via Agent Mail to split/release reservations so bd-us2k can proceed without overlap.","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:58:47Z"},{"id":981,"issue_id":"bd-us2k","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"PinkGorge completion evidence:
- Implemented expression-path ORDER BY/LIMIT/OFFSET post-processing in `crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs` via `execute_program_with_postprocess` + `ExpressionPostprocess` metadata.

NOTE: The original consolidated spec extract for this rollup lives in the bead comments.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T05:16:39.108097323Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T17:35:14.952857972Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T06:39:48.352457310Z","close_reason":"DUPLICATE: Superseded by finer-grained bd-7pxb (§12.10-12.12) + bd-1mrj (§12.13-12.14) + bd-16ov (§12.15-12.16) + bd-cfj0 (§12.17)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-vr4u","depends_on_id":"bd-1x2z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T05:17:08.826138926Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-vr4u","depends_on_id":"bd-257u","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T05:17:08.718894377Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-vr4u","depends_on_id":"bd-31t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T06:09:57.111399611Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":23,"issue_id":"bd-vr4u","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## §12.10-12.17 Transaction Control + ATTACH + EXPLAIN + VACUUM + PRAGMA + Expressions + Type Affinity + Time Travel

### Transaction Control (§12.10)
BEGIN [DEFERRED|IMMEDIATE|EXCLUSIVE|CONCURRENT]; COMMIT; END (=COMMIT); ROLLBACK.
SAVEPOINT name; RELEASE [SAVEPOINT] name; ROLLBACK TO [SAVEPOINT] name.

**Transaction modes:** DEFERRED (default, no locks until first read/write). IMMEDIATE (RESERVED lock immediately). EXCLUSIVE (EXCLUSIVE lock immediately; equivalent to IMMEDIATE in WAL mode). CONCURRENT (FrankenSQLite extension: MVCC concurrent writer mode with SI, conflicts on same page → SQLITE_BUSY_SNAPSHOT).

**Savepoints:** Stack-based. RELEASE X commits work since SAVEPOINT X and pops X + all more recent. ROLLBACK TO X undoes work since SAVEPOINT X but leaves X on stack.

### ATTACH/DETACH (§12.11)
`ATTACH expr AS schema-name; DETACH schema-name`. Main = "main", temp = "temp". Max 10 attached (SQLITE_MAX_ATTACHED). Cross-database transactions atomic only in rollback journal mode (standard SQLite); FrankenSQLite MUST support cross-database atomic WAL transactions via 2PC across WAL files.

### EXPLAIN (§12.12)
`EXPLAIN stmt` → VDBE bytecode (addr, opcode, p1-p5, comment).
`EXPLAIN QUERY PLAN stmt` → high-level plan (id, parent, notused, detail). Tree via id/parent.

### VACUUM (§12.13)
`VACUUM [schema]; VACUUM [schema] INTO filename`. Rebuilds database: create new, copy all, replace original. INTO = compact backup without modifying original.

### Other Statements (§12.14)
REINDEX [collation|table-or-index]. ANALYZE [schema|table-or-index] → sqlite_stat1/stat4.
PRAGMA [schema.]name [= value | (value)].

### Expression Syntax (§12.15)
Pratt parser (normative precedence in §10.2). Key: `NOT x = y` → `NOT (x = y)`. ESCAPE parsed as LIKE suffix. Unary binds tighter than COLLATE.

**Special forms:** CAST, CASE, EXISTS, IN, BETWEEN, COLLATE, LIKE/GLOB with ESCAPE, RAISE (trigger-only), JSON -> and ->> operators.

### Type Affinity Rules (§12.16)
Five affinities: TEXT, NUMERIC, INTEGER, REAL, BLOB.

**Comparison affinity rules:** (1) If one operand has INT/REAL/NUMERIC and other has TEXT/BLOB → apply numeric to TEXT/BLOB. (2) If one TEXT and other BLOB (no numeric) → apply TEXT to BLOB. (3) Same class or both BLOB → no conversion.

**Key distinction:** Affinity applied to operand needing conversion, not both. If both share class, no coercion.

### Time Travel Queries — Native Mode Extension (§12.17)
`SELECT ... FROM table FOR SYSTEM_TIME AS OF 'timestamp'` or `AS OF COMMITSEQ N`.

**Semantics:** (1) Determine target_commit_seq (binary search markers for timestamp, or direct for COMMITSEQ). (2) Create synthetic read-only snapshot S with S.high = target_commit_seq. (3) Execute query using normal MVCC resolution.

**Restrictions:** Time travel is read-only (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE/DDL → SQLITE_ERROR). History pruned → explicit error. Tiered storage: fetch symbols on demand under Cx budgets.
